title: Geopolitical tensions rock Strait of Hormuz as Spirit Airlines collapses and Buffett warns of speculative excess

blocks.content: Oil tankers were struck by unknown projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping chokepoint off the UAE, sending shockwaves through energy markets already on edge. Meanwhile, Spirit Airlines ceased operations overnight following a failed government bailout, marking one of the most significant collapses in US airline history.

For investors, the twin shocks of geopolitical disruption and structural airline failure underscore the fragile balance between supply constraints and market sentiment. Warren Buffett's warning that "we've never had people in a more gambling mood than now" adds another layer of caution as portfolio valuations face simultaneous pressure from multiple directions.
• S&P 500: down 0.8% as energy sector weakness weighs on broader sentiment
• FTSE 100: off 0.5% tracking European concerns over Middle East stability
• Nasdaq: sliding 1.1% as investors rotate away from risk assets
• STRAIT OF HORMUZ: A tanker was struck by unknown projectiles near Fujairah, prompting Trump to offer US assistance and sending oil prices higher before profits were taken.
• SPIRIT AIRLINES: The budget carrier ceased operations after its government bailout collapsed, stranding millions of passengers and creating chaos at US airports.
• BERKSHIRE HATHAWAY: Warren Buffett revealed a record $397 billion cash pile as operating earnings doubled, reassuring investors about corporate culture continuity under new leadership.
• ELON MUSK: The tech entrepreneur's lawsuit against OpenAI is being shaped by his high-profile clashes with a federal judge over the AI company's governance and mission.
OPEC+ holds its next meeting amid the Hormuz Strait crisis, with traders watching for signals on whether the cartel will adjust its output strategy in response to the disruption.
